replication_study.hypothesis: In the replication dataset, there is an interaction between ethnicity (European American vs. East Asian/Asian American) and priming condition (mortality salience vs. control) on Prostitution Attitudes Scale (PAS) scores: after mortality salience, Asian American participants will show higher (more positive) PAS scores and European American participants will show lower (less positive) PAS scores relative to the control condition.

data_plan.dataset_identifier: Ma-Kellams Replication Study Data.sav (SPSS file in replication_data)
data_plan.source_type: Experimental participant dataset (mortality-salience vs. control writing prime) collected for a direct replication; likely online sample (presence of Prolific indicator) and potentially mixed sources.
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Full replication dataset; robustness subsets include Prolific-only/non-Prolific-only and text-length filters (e.g., wrote >= 30 words).
data_plan.sample_size: Not specified in provided metadata; will be computed at runtime from the .sav file.
data_plan.access_details: Dataset provided in data/original/33/input/replication_data; to run, ensure the file is available at /app/data/Ma-Kellams Replication Study Data.sav inside the container.

qualification.explanation: This dataset closely reproduces the original study design (mortality-salience vs. control prime, PAS and bail outcomes) while constituting a new sample, thus testing robustness across an independent instance. Variable names and SPSS syntax reflect the same operationalizations, enabling a high-fidelity replication test.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Original paper (original_paper.pdf) Study 1 used a mortality-salience vs. dental-pain control writing task, followed by PAS and bail amount, analyzed with 2x2 ANOVAs (see pages 1020–1022, results F(1,38)=10.263, p=.003 for PAS interaction). The replication SPSS syntax (replication_data/Ma-Kellams Replication Analysis Syntax.sps) constructs PASAverage from PAS1, PAS2Positive, PAS3, PAS4, PAS5Positive, computes PANASNetScore, and runs UNIANOVA PASAverage BY Culture WritingCondition with Culture (EuropeanAmerican/EastAsian) and WritingCondition (0=control, 1=MS), matching the original analysis. The initial_details.txt also specifies the same focal claim and hypothesis.
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original data were laboratory-based student samples (post_registration.json: Study 1 N=42; original_paper.pdf: Study 1 participants and procedures). The replication dataset appears to include online participants (variable Prolific in the SPSS syntax) and additional text metrics (WordCount, SentenceCount), and provides robustness filters not present in the original. Ethnicity is coded via CulturalBackground then recoded into Culture (EuropeanAmerican vs. EastAsian) per the SPSS syntax; the original reported European American vs. Asian American. These differences indicate an independent sample with similar design conditions. Sources: post_registration.json (original sample sizes and measures), original_paper.pdf (study design and outcomes), and the SPSS syntax file (replication design and variables).

data_plan.notes: PAS items are Likert -3 to +3; PAS2 and PAS5 are reverse-scored into PAS2Positive and PAS5Positive, then averaged (SPSS syntax). BailAmount may be skewed; SPSS syntax applies outlier removal and log transformation for robustness. Culture is derived from CulturalBackground codes (1=EuropeanAmerican; 4=EastAsian). WritingCondition is coded 0=control, 1=MS. Additional variables include PANAS1–20 (delay/mood), WordCount, SentenceCount, Prolific, Religion, BornCountry. Ensure the .sav file is present at /app/data and encoded properly for pyreadstat.

steps: Load the SPSS .sav dataset from /app/data using pyreadstat.

Construct variables: Wrote30WordsOrMore from WordCount; EuropeanAmerican and EastAsian from CulturalBackground; Culture=0 for EuropeanAmerican, 1 for EastAsian; PAS2Positive and PAS5Positive by reverse-scoring; PASAverage as the mean of PAS1, PAS2Positive, PAS3, PAS4, PAS5Positive; PANASNetScore as sum(positive PANAS items) - sum(negative PANAS items).
Primary analysis: Run a Type III two-way ANOVA (sum-to-zero contrasts) with PASAverage as the DV and Culture, WritingCondition, and their interaction as factors.
Compute cell means (mean, count, SE) for PASAverage by Culture x WritingCondition.

Simple-effects analyses: (a) within European Americans: PASAverage ~ WritingCondition; (b) within East Asians: PASAverage ~ WritingCondition; (c) cultural differences within each condition: PASAverage ~ Culture (control only; mortality only).

Secondary (robustness) analyses mirroring SPSS syntax: mood effects (PANASNetScore main and interaction with WritingCondition), bail analyses (with outlier removal and log transformation), exclude short responses (>=30 words), exclude low PAS outliers, subset by Prolific, subset by non-religious.
Save ANOVA tables and cell means to /app/data and write a summary log.
planned_method.models: Two-way ANOVA (Type III sums of squares) for PASAverage with fixed factors Culture and WritingCondition; simple-effects ANOVAs within subsets; additional ANOVAs for bail and robustness checks.
planned_method.outcome_variable: PASAverage (mean of PAS1, PAS2Positive, PAS3, PAS4, PAS5Positive; higher = more positive attitudes toward prostitution).
planned_method.independent_variables: Culture (0=European American, 1=East Asian) and WritingCondition (0=control, 1=mortality salience).
planned_method.control_variables: None in the focal test; PANASNetScore and Religion used in robustness checks mirroring SPSS syntax; bail outcome analyzed separately.
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.10; pyreadstat for reading SPSS; pandas and numpy for data processing; statsmodels (with sum contrasts) for Type III ANOVA.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: F-statistic for the Culture x WritingCondition interaction effect on PASAverage (Type III ANOVA), plus cell means and simple-effect F-tests.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: ANOVA F-tests (Type III sums of squares); alpha=0.05, two-tailed. Evaluate the sign/pattern of simple effects to confirm crossover.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion per model (drop rows with missing DV or factors); replace inf with NaN before analysis.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Not applicable for the focal hypothesis; robustness checks are clearly labeled as exploratory without multiplicity correction.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Primary success if the Culture x WritingCondition interaction on PASAverage is significant (p<0.05) and shows the original directionality: EA lower PAS under mortality vs. control; AA higher PAS under mortality vs. control. Report effect sizes and consistency of simple effects.

files.replication_data/Ma-Kellams Replication: Python translation of the provided SPSS syntax. It loads /app/data/Ma-Kellams Replication Study Data.sav via pyreadstat; constructs PASAverage, Culture, PANASNetScore, and other variables; runs Type III ANOVAs with sum contrasts for the focal 2x2 on PAS, computes cell means, and runs robustness/simple-effects analyses. Outputs ANOVA tables and cell means as CSVs and writes replication_results_summary.txt to /app/data.

codebase.notes: All file I/O uses /app/data. Ensure the SPSS dataset is present at /app/data/Ma-Kellams Replication Study Data.sav before execution. The script saves outputs like anova_pas_culture_x_condition.csv, cell_means_pas.csv, and replication_results_summary.txt. Dependencies: pyreadstat, pandas, numpy, statsmodels.

instructions: Place Ma-Kellams Replication Study Data.sav at ./data so it is mounted to /app/data inside the container.
Run: python /app/data/original/33/input/replication_data/Ma-Kellams Replication Analysis Syntax__py.py (or copy the script into the container and execute it).
Inspect outputs in /app/data: anova_pas_culture_x_condition.csv (focal test), cell_means_pas.csv, replication_results_summary.txt, and any robustness outputs.
comparison_metrics: Significance and magnitude of the Culture x WritingCondition interaction F on PASAverage (compare to original F(1,38)=10.263, p=.003; initial_details.txt and original_paper.pdf).
Directionality of simple effects: EA PAS lower in mortality vs. control; AA PAS higher in mortality vs. control (post_registration.json numerical results and original_paper.pdf Study 1).
Pattern of cell means (crossover) and convergence under mortality-salience (original_paper.pdf Figure 1).
